title: Service JWT Payload (HS256)
description: The payload shape for the short-lived HS256 JWT used on the Seam 2 (inbound trust) service-to-service RPC path. Verification order is fixed at 13 steps — see the companion service-jwt.v1.md document.
$comment: JSON Schema draft 2020-12. Seam 2 (Inbound trust). Signature: HS256 over a >=32-byte Doppler secret (SERVICE_JWT_SIGNING_KEY). Producer: any service that authored the token (self-identified in 'iss'). Consumer: the service named in 'aud'. See ADR-0022 D2 for the seam definition; see service-jwt.v1.md for the verification steps.

iss.description: Issuer — the service slug of the caller. Matches the service_account.slug of the calling service. Verification step 9 rejects an empty issuer.

aud.description: Audience — the service slug of the intended receiver. The receiver verifies aud == its own slug (verification step 8). A token addressed to 'ai-service' is refused by any other consumer.

sub.description: Subject — the ServiceAccount ID of the caller. 'sacc_' prefixed ULID.

iat.description: Issued-at time as a Unix epoch integer (seconds since 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z). Verification step 5 rejects iat > now + 30s (clock skew tolerance).

exp.description: Expiration time as a Unix epoch integer. Every token expires within 60 seconds of iat — Seam 2 tokens are short-lived by design. Verification step 6 rejects exp < now - 30s.

jti.description: JWT ID — a fresh Crockford ULID (26 chars) per token. Used for replay-prevention nonce tracking. Verification step 13 (optional) rejects duplicate JTIs seen within the exp window.

tenant_id.description: The tenant the caller is acting on behalf of. Every Seam 2 token is tenant-scoped. Verification step 11 rejects tokens with a missing or empty tenant_id.
tenant_id.$comment: Contract Rule 4 — every cross-service token is tenant-scoped. Central-plane admin operations use a distinct platform-admin PAT with tenant_id: null in the identity envelope, and NEVER use the Seam 2 JWT path. See ADR-0022 D4 + docs/contracts/README.md §Rule 4.

on_behalf_of_user_id.description: Optional. The tenant user whose intent triggered this cross-service call. Downstream services log it for audit + can enforce user-level policies on top of the service-level authorization.

abilities.description: Optional subset of the caller's ability list scoped to this specific token. Empty or absent = the caller's full ability list applies. Populated when the caller wants to narrow a specific call to a subset of its abilities (defense-in-depth).

correlation_id.description: Optional X-Correlation-Id echo. When present, the receiver adopts this correlation id for its log lines + spans; when absent, the receiver reads the id from the X-Correlation-Id HTTP header.
